ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for wholemeal bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter; mapped to broader bread food product class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — wholemeal/wholegrain bread composite
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for wholemeal bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter; A000E (bread and similar products) used as the closest defensible parent anchor. The composite nature of this food (bread + chocolate spread + peanut butter) is not captured by any single FoodEx2 code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite bread-spread food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is approximately 100 g, equivalent to two medium slices of wholemeal bread each spread with chocolate spread and peanut butter.

nutrient_source.title: Wholemeal bread with chocolate and peanut butter — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for wholemeal bread with chocolate and peanut butter (cr_id F05020137). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central component analogy (wholemeal bread + chocolate hazelnut spread + peanut butter weighted proportions) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Wholemeal Bread — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous composite estimate for wholemeal bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 69 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wholemeal bread (GI ~69, medium category). The addition of peanut butter (low GI, ~14) and chocolate spread (moderate GI contribution) results in a composite GI estimated at 69 for this assembled food. Glycemic load per 100 g serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate g per serving) / 100 = (69 × 40.21) / 100 ≈ 27.7. This is consistent with the input record value of 27.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 309 mg/100 g combined with sugar 12.79 g/100 g; sugar intake independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one slice (approximately 50 g) to keep sodium intake below 155 mg per serving, and should replace chocolate spread with an unsweetened nut butter to eliminate the sugar-driven blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 12.79 g/100 g from chocolate spread combined with medium GI of 69 and glycemic load of 27.7 per 100 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should reduce or eliminate the chocolate spread to lower the sugar content and glycemic load; wholemeal bread with peanut butter alone is a more suitable combination due to the fibre (5.21 g/100 g) and protein (13.15 g/100 g) moderating postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 3.5 g/100 g, sodium 309 mg/100 g, and sugar 12.79 g/100 g; peanut butter contributes beneficial unsaturated fats and dietary fibre 5.21 g/100 g is cardioprotective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, choose natural peanut butter without added palm oil or hydrogenated fats, reduce or eliminate chocolate spread to lower saturated fat and sugar, and limit portion to one slice to manage sodium intake within daily cardiovascular targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wholemeal bread with chocolate spread and peanut butter is a composite bread-based dish consisting of sliced wholemeal (whole wheat) bread topped with a chocolate hazelnut-style spread and peanut butter. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast item or snack in Singapore and internationally. The wholemeal bread provides a dense, slightly nutty texture and a mildly earthy flavour from the whole grain, while the chocolate spread contributes sweetness and a creamy cocoa flavour, and the peanut butter adds a rich, savoury-sweet nuttiness with a thick, spreadable texture. Nutritionally, this food delivers 338 kcal per 100 g, with a meaningful protein content of 13.15 g, moderate fat at 14.27 g (predominantly unsaturated from peanut butter), and 40.21 g of carbohydrates. The dietary fibre content of 5.21 g per 100 g is notable and supports satiety and gut health. However, the sugar content of 12.79 g per 100 g — largely attributable to the chocolate spread — is a clinical concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Sodium at 309 mg per 100 g is borderline elevated. The glycemic index is estimated at 69 (medium category) with a glycemic load of 27.7 per 100 g serving, indicating a moderate postprandial glucose impact. Iron content is high at 4.47 mg per 100 g, and calcium at 148 mg per 100 g is notable. This food is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal-compliant. It contains gluten and peanuts as major allergens. For health-conscious consumers, replacing the chocolate spread with an unsweetened nut butter or reducing its quantity significantly improves the nutritional profile.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020137, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central component analogy and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database wholemeal bread entry with documented analogous basis. All values are internally consistent and plausible.
notes: Saturated fat of 3.5 g/100 g is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central component analogy (wholemeal bread + chocolate hazelnut spread + peanut butter in approximate proportions); HPB does not report saturated fat for this composite entry. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo database for this composite entry. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero for standard commercial wholemeal bread and peanut butter formulations per USDA FoodData Central. water_g set to -1: moisture data not reported by HPB for this entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields not available in the source record for this composite food. GI value of 69 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database wholemeal bread entry; the composite GI may be slightly lower due to the fat and protein contribution of peanut butter. The input health_suitability cautions incorrectly labelled this solid food as a 'sugar-sweetened beverage'; this error has been corrected in the output health_context. This food is not a Singaporean-specific dish and therefore the multilingual naming requirement for Singaporean dishes (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) does not apply; the food is classified as Western/International. FoodEx2 match_type corrected from 'analogous_estimate' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].
